title: Cables, Connectors, Adapters, Interfaces, and Capability Negotiation · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic cables, connectors, adapters, interfaces, and capability negotiation ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in connector and end-to-end capability map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.
Compare category, conductor, shielding, length, bend, rating, directionality, active components, markings, and environmental suitability.
Distinguish passive pin adaptation from active protocol conversion and account for direction, resolution, bandwidth, audio, copy protection, and power.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Connector Identification
steps.instruction:
debug case — Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.

Inspect the Connector Identification trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Connector Identification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify common usb, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Connector Identification, keep Connector Identification connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Connector Identification, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Connector Identification, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Connector Identification precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Identify common USB, video, storage, network, audio, power, serial, and legacy connector form factors without inferring capability from shape alone.

For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Connector Identification, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Two cables with the same connector shape have the same data, video, and power capabilities.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Signal Path Test has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Signal Path Test, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Signal Path Test precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Signal Path Test, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring.

For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Signal Path Test, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Replacing every cable and adapter simultaneously is the fastest way to identify cause.

Change one CompTIA A+ support user, device, version, hazard, symptom, access need, or competing cause; verify repair, rollback, original and changed cases, prevention, handoff, and controlled equipment transfer limits for the debug Signal Path Test case

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Signal Path Test
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Signal Path Test: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Look-alike USB-C cables and adapters expose different data, video, direction, bandwidth, and negotiated-power behavior through one dock. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Signal Path Test.
steps.why: Retrieving Signal Path Test through the concrete connector and end-to-end capability map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Signal Path Test, keep Signal Path Test connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test a signal path by substituting one known-good bounded layer, recording negotiated capability, and avoiding unsafe improvised wiring.
